author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XI. 1–54.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among all the miracles wrought by our Lord Jesus Christ, the resurrection of Lazarus holds a foremost place in preaching.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we consider attentively who did it, our duty is to rejoice rather than to wonder.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A man was raised up by Him who made man: for He is the only One of the Father, by whom, as you know, all things were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if all things were made by Him, what wonder is it that one was raised by Him, when so many are daily brought into the world by His power?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a greater deed to create men than to raise them again from the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet He deigned both to create and to raise again; to create all, to resuscitate some.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For though the Lord Jesus did many such acts, yet all of them are not recorded; just as this same St. John the evangelist himself testifies, that Christ the Lord both said and did many things that are not recorded; but such were chosen for record as seemed to suffice for the salvation of believers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast just heard that the Lord Jesus raised a dead man to life; and that is sufficient to let thee know that, were He so pleased, He might raise all the dead to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, indeed this very work has He reserved in His own hands till the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For while you have heard that by a great miracle He raised one from the tomb who had been dead four days, “the hour is coming,” as He Himself saith, “in the which all that are in the graves shall hear His voice, and shall come forth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He raised one who was putrid, and yet in that putrid carcase there was still the form of limbs; but at the last day He will by a word reconstitute ashes into human flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it was needful then to do only some such deeds, that we, receiving them as tokens of His power, may put our trust in Him, and be preparing for that resurrection which shall be to life and not to judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, indeed, He saith, “The hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear His voice, and shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have, however, read in the Gospel of three dead persons who were raised to life by the Lord, and, let us hope, to some good purpose.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For surely the Lord’s deeds are not merely deeds, but signs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if they are signs, besides their wonderful character, they have some real significance: and to find out this in regard to such deeds is a somewhat harder task than to read or hear of them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We were listening with wonder, as at the sight of some mighty miracle enacted before our eyes, in the reading of the Gospel, how Lazarus was restored to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we turn our thoughts to the still more wonderful works of Christ, every one that believeth riseth again: if we all consider, and understand that more horrifying kind of death, every one who sinneth dies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But every man is afraid of the death of the flesh; few, of the death of the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In regard to the death of the flesh, which must certainly come some time, all are on their guard against its approach: this is the source of all their labor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Man, destined to die, labors to avert his dying; and yet man, destined to live for ever, labors not to cease from sinning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when he labors to avoid dying, he labors to no purpose, for its only result will be to put off death for a while, not to escape it; but if he refrain from sinning, his toil will cease, and he shall live for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh that we could arouse men, and be ourselves aroused along with them, to be as great lovers of the life that abideth, as men are of that which passeth away!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What will a man not do who is placed under the peril of death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the sword was overhanging their heads, men have given up every means of living they had in reserve.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is there that has not made an immediate surrender of all, to escape being slain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, after all, he has perhaps been slain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is there that, to save his life, has not been willing at once to lose his means of living, and prefer a life of beggary to a speedy death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who has had it said to him, Be off to sea if you would escape with your life, and has delayed to do so?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who has had it said to him, Set to work if you would preserve your life, and has continued a sluggard?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is but little that God requires of us, that we may live for ever: and we neglect to obey Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God says not to thee, Lose all you have, that you may live a little time oppressed with toil; but, Give to the poor of what you have, that you may live always exempt from labor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The lovers of this temporal life, which is theirs, neither when, nor as long as they wish, are our accusers; and we accuse not ourselves in turn, so sluggish are we, so lukewarm about obtaining eternal life, which will be ours if we wish it, and will be imperishable when we have it; but this death which we fear, notwithstanding all our reluctance, will yet be ours in possession.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the Lord in the greatness of His grace and mercy raiseth our souls to life, that we may not die for ever, we may well understand that those three dead persons whom He raised in the body, have some figurative significance of that resurrection of the soul which is effected by faith: He raised up the ruler of the synagogue’s daughter, while still lying in the house; He raised up the widow’s young son, while being carried outside the gates of the city; and He raised up Lazarus, when four days in the grave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let each one give heed to his own soul: in sinning he dies: sin is the death of the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But sometimes sin is committed only in thought.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast felt delight in what is evil, thou hast assented to its commission, thou hast sinned; that assent has slain thee: but the death is internal, because the evil thought had not yet ripened into action.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord intimated that He would raise such a soul to life, in raising that girl, who had not yet been carried forth to the burial, but was lying dead in the house, as if sin still lay concealed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou hast not only harbored a feeling of delight in evil, but hast also done the evil thing, thou hast, so to speak, carried the dead outside the gate: thou art already without, and being carried to the tomb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet such an one also the Lord raised to life. and restored to his widowed mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast sinned, repent, and the Lord will raise thee up, and restore thee to thy mother Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The third example of death is Lazarus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A grievous kind of death it is, and is distinguished as a habit of wickedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is one thing to fall into sin, another to form the habit of sinning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who falls into sin, and straightway submits to correction, will be speedily restored to life; for he is not yet entangled in the habit, he is not yet laid in the tomb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he who has become habituated to sin, is buried, and has it properly said of him, “he stinketh;” for his character, like some horrible smell, begins to be of the worst repute.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such are all who are habituated to crime, abandoned in morals.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou sayest to such an one, Do not so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when wilt thou be listened to by one on whom the earth is thus heaped, who is breeding corruption, and pressed down with the weight of habit?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet the power of Christ was not unequal to the task of restoring such an one to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know, we have seen, we see every day men changing the very worst of habits, and adopting a better manner of life than that of those who blamed them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou detestedst such a man: look at the sister of Lazarus herself (if, indeed, it was she who anointed the Lord’s feet with ointment, and wiped with her hair what she had washed with her tears), who had a better resurrection than her brother; she was delivered from the mighty burden of a sinful character.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For she was a notorious sinner; and had it said of her, “Her many sins are forgiven her, for she has loved much.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We see many such, we know many: let none despair, but let none presume in himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both the one and the other are sinful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let thine unwillingness to despair take such a turn as to lead thee to make choice of Him in whom alone thou mayest well presume.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So then the Lord also raised Lazarus to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard what type of character he represents; in other words, what is meant by the resurrection of Lazarus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us now, therefore, read over the passage; and as there is much in this lesson clear already, we shall not go into any detailed exposition, so as to take up more thoroughly the necessary points.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now a certain man was sick, [named] Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and Martha, his sisters.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the previous lesson you remember that the Lord escaped from the hands of those who sought to stone Him, and went away beyond Jordan, where John baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord therefore had taken up His abode there, Lazarus fell sick in Bethany, which was a town lying close to Jerusalem.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But Mary was she who anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped His feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore his sisters sent unto Him, saying.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We now understand whither it was they sent, namely, where the Lord was; for He was away, as you know, beyond the Jordan.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They sent messengers to the Lord to tell Him that their brother was ill.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He delayed to heal, that He might be able to raise to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what was the message sent by his sisters?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Lord, behold, he whom Thou lovest is sick.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They did not say, Come; for the intimation was all that was needed for one who loved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They did not venture to say, Come and heal him: they ventured not to say, Command there, and it shall be done here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why not so with them, if on these very grounds the centurion’s faith was commended?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he said, “I am not worthy that Thou shouldest enter under my roof; but speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No such words said these women, but only, “Lord, behold, he whom Thou lovest is sick.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is enough that Thou knowest; for Thou art not one that loveth and forsaketh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But says some one, How could a sinner be represented by Lazarus, and be so loved by the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him listen to Him, when He says, “I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had not God loved sinners, He would not have come down from heaven to earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But when Jesus heard [that], He said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such a glorifying of Himself did not add to His dignity, but benefited us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence He says, “is not unto death,” because even that death itself was not unto death, but rather unto the working of a miracle whereby men might be led to faith in Christ, and so escape the real death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And mark how the Lord, as it were indirectly, called Himself God, for the sake of some who deny that the Son is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there are heretics who make such a denial, that the Son of God is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them hearken here: “This sickness,” He says, “is not unto death, but for the glory of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what glory?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the glory of what God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what follows: “That the Son of God may be glorified.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This sickness,” therefore, He says, “is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God maybe glorified thereby.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By that sickness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister Mary, and Lazarus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one sick, the others sad, all of them beloved: but He who loved them was both the Saviour of the sick, nay more, the Raiser of the dead and the Comforter of the sad.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When He heard therefore that he was sick, He abode then two days still in the same place.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They sent Him word: He abode where He was: and the time ran on till four days were completed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And not in vain, were it only that perhaps, nay that certainly, even the very number of days has some sacramental significance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then after that He saith again to His disciples, Let us go into Judea:” where He had been all but stoned, and from which He had apparently departed for the very purpose to escape being stoned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as man He departed; but returned as if in forgetfulness of all infirmity, to show His power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Let us go,” He said, “into Judea.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now see how the disciples were terrified at His words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The disciples say unto Him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone Thee, and goest Thou thither again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “What means such an answer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They said to Him, “The Jews of late sought to stone Thee, and goest Thou thither again” to be stoned?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord, “Are there not twelve hours in the day? if any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world: but if he walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He spoke indeed of the day, but to our understanding as if it were still the night.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us call upon the Day to chase away the night, and illuminate our hearts with the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what did the Lord mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As far as I can judge, and as the height and depth of His meaning breaks into light, He wished to argue down their doubting and unbelief.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they wished by their counsel to keep the Lord from death, who had come to die, to save themselves from death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a similar way also, in another passage, St. Peter, who loved the Lord, but did not yet fully understand the reason of His coming, was afraid of His dying, and so displeased the Life, to wit, the Lord Himself; for when He was intimating to the disciples what He was about to suffer at Jerusalem at the hands of the Jews, Peter made reply among the rest, and said, “Far be it from Thee, Lord; pity Thyself: this shall not be unto Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And at once the Lord replied, “Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savorest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet a little before, in confessing the Son of God, he had merited commendation: for he heard the words, “Blessed art thou, Simon Barjona: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom He had said, “Blessed art thou,” He now says, “Get thee behind me, Satan;” because it was not of himself that he was blessed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But of what then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, this is how thou art blessed, not from anything that is thine own, but from that which is mine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that I am the Father, but that all things which the Father hath are mine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if his blessedness came from the Lord’s own working, from whose [working] came he to be Satan?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He there tells us: for He assigned the reason of such blessedness, when He said, “Flesh and blood hath not revealed this unto thee, but my Father who is in heaven:” that is the cause of thy blessedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that I said, “Get thee behind me, Satan, hear also its cause.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou savorest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no one then flatter himself: in that which is natural to himself he is Satan, in that which is of God he is blessed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all that is of his own, whence comes it, but from his sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Put away the sin, which is thine own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Righteousness, He saith, belongeth unto me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what hast thou that thou didst not receive?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when men wished to give counsel to God, disciples to their Master, servants to their Lord, patients to their Physician, He reproved them by saying, “Are there not twelve hours in the day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Follow me, if ye would not stumble: give not counsel to me, from whom you ought to receive it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what, then, refer the words, “Are there not twelve hours in the day”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just that to point Himself out as the day, He made choice of twelve disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I am the day, He says, and you the hours, is it for the hours to give counsel to the day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The day is followed by the hours, not the hours by the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If these, then, were the hours, what in such a reckoning was Judas?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was he also among the twelve hours?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he was an hour, he had light; and if he had light, how was the Day betrayed by him to death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord, in so speaking, foresaw, not Judas himself, but his successor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Judas, when he fell, was succeeded by Matthias, and the duodenary number preserved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not, then, without a purpose that the Lord made choice of twelve disciples, but to indicate that He Himself is the spiritual Day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the hours then attend upon the Day, let them preach the Day, be made known and illuminated by the Day, and by the preaching of the hours may the world believe in the Day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so in a summary way it was just this that He said: Follow me, if ye would not stumble.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And after that He saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was true what He said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To his sisters he was dead, to the Lord he was asleep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was dead to men, who could not raise him again; but the Lord aroused him with as great ease from the tomb as one arouseth a sleeper from his bed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence it was in reference to His own power that He spoke of him as sleeping: for others also, who are dead, are frequently spoken of in Scripture as sleeping; as when the apostle says, “But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning those who are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as others who have no hope.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he also spoke of them as sleeping, because foretelling their resurrection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, all the dead are sleeping, both good and bad.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But just as, in the case of those who sleep and waken day by day, there is a great difference as to what they severally see in their sleep: some experience pleasant dreams; others, dreams so frightful that the waking are afraid to fall asleep for fear of their recurrence: so every individual sleeps and wakens in circumstances peculiar to himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there is a difference as to the kind of custody one may be placed in, who is afterwards to be taken before the judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the kind of custody in which men are placed depends on the merits of the case: some are required to be guarded by lictors, an office humane and mild, and becoming a citizen; others are given up to subordinates; some, again, are sent to prison: and in the prison itself all are not thrust together into its lowest dungeons, but dealt with in proportion to the merits and superior gravity of the charges.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As, then, there are different kinds of custody among those engaged in official life, so there are different kinds of custody for the dead, and differing merits in those who rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The beggar was taken into custody, so was the rich man: but the one into Abraham’s bosom; the other, where he thirsted, and found not a drop of water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, to make this the occasion of instructing your Charity, all souls have, when they quit this world, their different receptions.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The good have joy; the evil, torments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the resurrection takes place, both the joy of the good will be fuller and the torments of the wicked heavier, when they shall be tormented in the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The holy patriarchs, prophets, apostles, martyrs, and good believers, have been received into peace; but all of them have still in the end to receive the fulfillment of the divine promises; for they have been promised also the resurrection of the flesh, the destruction of death, and eternal life with the angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This we have all to receive together; for the rest, which is given immediately after death, every one, if worthy of it, receives when he dies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The patriarchs first received it—think only from what they rest; the prophets afterwards; more recently the apostles; still more lately the holy martyrs, and day by day the good and faithful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus some have now been in that rest for long, some not so long; others for fewer years, and others whose entrance therein is still less than recent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when they shall wake from this sleep, they shall all together receive the fulfillment of the promise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said His disciples”—according to their understanding they replied—“Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the sleep of the sick is usually a sign of returning health.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Howbeit Jesus spake of his death, but they thought that He spake of the taking of rest in sleep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said Jesus unto them plainly,”—for He said somewhat obscurely, “He sleepeth;”—therefore He said plainly, “Lazarus is dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I even know that he is dead, and I was not there: for he had been reported not as dead, but sick.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what could remain hid from Him who had created it, and into whose hands the soul of the dying man had departed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is why He said, “I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe;” that they might now begin to wonder that the Lord could assert his death, which He had neither seen nor heard of.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For here we ought specially to bear in mind that as yet the disciples themselves, who already believed in Him, had their faith built up by miracles: not that a faith, utterly wanting till then, might begin to exist; but that what had previously come into being might be increased; although He made use of such an expression as if only then they would begin to believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He said not, “I am glad for your sakes,” that your faith may be increased or confirmed; but, “that ye may believe;” which is to be understood as meaning, that your faith may be fuller and more vigorous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Nevertheless, let us go unto him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said Thomas, who is called Didymus, unto his fellow disciples, Let us also go, that we may die with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore Jesus came, and found that he had [lain] in the grave four days already.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Much might be said of the four days, according to the wont of the obscure passages of Scripture, which bear as many senses as there is diversity of those who understand them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us express also our opinion of what is meant by one four days dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as in the former case of the blind man we understand in a way the human race, so in the case of this dead man many perhaps are also to be understood; for one thing may be signified by different figures.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When a man is born, he is born already in a state of death; for he inherits sin from Adam.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence the apostle says: “By one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin; and so that passed upon all men, wherein all have sinned.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here you have one day of death because man inherits it from the seed stock of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thereafter he grows, and begins to approach the years of reason that he may know the law of nature, which every one has had implanted in his heart: What thou wouldst not have done to thyself, do not to another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is this learned from the pages of a book, and not in a measure legible in our very nature?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou any desire to be robbed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See here, then, the law in thy heart: What thou art unwilling to suffer, be unwilling to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This law also is transgressed by men; and here, then, we have the second day of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The law was also divinely given through Moses, the servant of God; and therein it is said, “Thou shalt not kill; thou shalt not commit adultery; thou shalt not bear false witness; honor thy father and mother; thou shalt not covet thy neighbor’s property; thou shalt not covet thy neighbor’s wife.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here you have the written law, and it also is despised: this is the third day of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What remains?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The gospel also comes, the kingdom of heaven is preached, Christ is everywhere published; He threatens hell, He promises eternal life; and that also is despised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Men transgress the gospel; and this is the fourth day of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now he deservedly stinketh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But is mercy to be denied to such?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God forbid; for to raise such also from the dead, the Lord thinks it not unfitting to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And many of the Jews had come to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met Him; but Mary sat [still] in the house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if Thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I know that even now, whatsoever Thou wilt ask of God, God will give it Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She did not say, But even now I ask Thee to raise my brother to life again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how could she know if such a resurrection would be of benefit to her brother?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She only said, I know that Thou canst, and whatsoever Thou art pleased, Thou doest: for Thy doing it is dependent on Thine own judgment, not on my presumption.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But even now I know that, whatsoever Thou wilt ask of God, God will give it Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was ambiguous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He said not, Even now I will raise thy brother; but, “Thy brother shall rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Martha saith unto Him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection, at the last day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that resurrection I am sure, but uncertain about this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto her, I am the resurrection.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou sayest, My brother shall rise again at the last day: true; but by Him, through whom he shall rise then, can he rise even now, for “I,” He says, “am the resurrection and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give ear, brethren, give ear to what He says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly the universal expectation of the bystanders was that Lazarus, one who had been dead four days, would live again; let us hear, and rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many are there in this audience who are crushed down under the weighty mass of some sinful habit!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps some are hearing me to whom it may be said, “Be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess;” and they say, We cannot.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some others, it may be, are hearing me, who are unclean, and stained with lusts and crimes, and to whom it is said, Refrain from such conduct, that ye perish not; and they reply, We cannot give up our habits.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O Lord, raise them again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am,” He says, “the resurrection and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The resurrection because the life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live: and whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth in me, though he were dead,” just as Lazarus is dead, “yet shall he live;” for He is not the God of the dead, but of the living.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such was the answer He gave the Jews concerning their fathers, long ago dead, that is, concerning Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living; for all live unto Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believe then, and though thou wert dead, yet shalt thou live: but if thou believest not, even while thou livest thou art dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us prove this likewise, that if thou believest not, though thou livest thou art dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To one who was delaying to follow Him, and saying, “Let me first go and bury my father,” the Lord said, “Let the dead bury their dead; but come thou and follow me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There was there a dead man requiring to be buried, there were there also dead men to bury the dead: the one was dead in the flesh, the others in soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how comes death on the soul?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When faith is wanting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How comes death on the body?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the soul is wanting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore thy soul’s soul is faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth in me,” says Christ, though he were dead in the flesh, yet shall he live in the spirit; till the flesh also rise again, never more to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is “he that believeth in me,” though he die, “yet shall he live.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whosoever liveth” in the flesh, “and believeth in me,” though he shall die in time on account of the death of the flesh, “shall never die,” because of the life of the spirit, and the immortality of the resurrection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such is the meaning of the words, “And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believest thou this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She saith unto Him, Yea, Lord, I have believed that Thou art the Christ, the Son of God, who hast come into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When I believed this, I believed that Thou art the resurrection, that Thou art the life: I believed that he that believeth in Thee, though he die, yet shall he live; and whosoever liveth and believeth in Thee, shall never die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister silently, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is worthy of notice the way in which the whispering of her voice was denominated silence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how could she be silent, when she said, “The Master is come, and calleth for thee”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is also to be noticed why it is that the evangelist has not said where, or when, or how the Lord called for Mary; namely, that in order to preserve the brevity of the narrative, it may rather be understood from the words of Martha.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was still in that place where Martha met Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews, then, who were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily, and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave, to weep there.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What cause had the evangelist to tell us this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To show us what it was that occasioned the numerous concourse of people to be there when Lazarus was raised to life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Jews, thinking that her reason for hastening away was to seek in weeping the solace of her grief, followed her; that the great miracle of one rising again who had been four days dead, might have the presence of many witnesses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw Him, she fell down at His feet, saying unto Him, Lord, if Thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping, who were with her, He groaned in the spirit, and troubled Himself, and said, Where have ye laid him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Something there is, did we but know it, that He has suggested to us by groaning in the spirit, and troubling Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who could trouble Him, save He Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, first give heed here to the power that did so, and then look for the meaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art troubled against thy will; Christ was troubled because He willed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus hungered, it is true, but because He willed; Jesus slept, it is true, but because He willed; He was sorrowful, it is true, but because He willed; He died, it is true, but because He willed: in His own power it lay to be thus and thus affected or not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Word assumed soul and flesh, fitting on Himself our whole human nature in the oneness of His person.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the soul of the apostle was illuminated by the Word; so was the soul of Peter, the soul of Paul, of the other apostles, and the holy prophets,—the souls of all were illuminated by the Word; but of none was it said, “The Word was made flesh;” of none was it said,” I and the Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul and flesh of Christ is one person with the Word of God, one Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And by this [Word] wherein resided the supreme power, was infirmity made use of at the beck of His will; and in this way “He troubled Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have spoken of the power: look now to the meaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a great criminal that is signified by that four days’ death and burial.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why is it, then, that Christ troubleth Himself, but to intimate to thee how thou oughtest to be troubled, when weighed down and crushed by so great a mass of iniquity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For here thou hast been looking to thyself, been seeing thine own guilt, been reckoning for thyself: I have done this, and God has spared me; I have committed this, and He hath borne with me; I have heard the gospel, and despised it; I have been baptized, and returned again to the same course: what am I doing? whither am I going? how shall I escape?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou speakest thus, Christ is already groaning; for thy faith is groaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the voice of one who groaneth thus, there comes to light the hope of his rising again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If such faith is within, there is Christ groaning; for if there is faith in us, Christ is in us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what else says the apostle: “That Christ may dwell in your hearts by faith.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore thy faith in Christ is Christ Himself in thy heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is why He slept in the ship; and why, when His disciples were in danger and already on the verge of shipwreck, they came to Him and awoke Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ arose, laid His commands on the winds and waves, and there ensued a great calm.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also with thee; the winds enter thy heart, that is, where thou sailest, where thou passest along this life as a stormy and dangerous sea; the winds enter, the billows rise and toss thy vessel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are the winds?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast received some insult, and art wroth: that insult is the wind; that anger, the waves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art in danger, thou preparest to reply, to render cursing for cursing, and thy vessel is already nigh to shipwreck.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Awake the Christ who is sleeping.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou art in commotion, and making ready to render evil for evil, because Christ is sleeping in thy vessel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the sleep of Christ in thy heart is the forgetfulness of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou arousest Christ, that is, recallest thy faith, what dost thou hear said to thee by Christ, when now awake in thy heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I [He says] have heard it said to me, “Thou hast a devil,” and I have prayed for them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord hears and suffers; the servant hears and is angry!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou wishest to be avenged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am already avenged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thy faith so speaks to thee, command is exercised, as it were, over the winds and waves, and there is a great calm.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As, then, to awaken Christ in the vessel is just to awaken faith; so in the heart of one who is pressed down by a great mass and habit of sin, in the heart of the man who has been a transgressor even of the holy gospel and a despiser of eternal punishment, let Christ groan, let such a man betake himself to self-accusation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear still more: Christ wept; let man bemoan himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For why did Christ weep, but to teach man to weep?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore did He groan and trouble Himself, but to intimate that the faith of one who has just cause to be displeased with himself ought to be in a sense groaning over the accusation of wicked works, to the end that the habit of sinning may give way to the vehemence of penitential sorrow?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And He said, Where have ye laid him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou knewest that he was dead, and art Thou ignorant of the place of his burial?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The meaning here is, that a man thus lost becomes, as it were, unknown to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have not ventured to say, Is unknown—for what is unknown to Him? but, As it were unknown.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how do we prove this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the Lord, who will yet say in the judgment, “I know you not: depart from me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does that mean, “I know you not”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see you not in that light of mine—in that righteousness which I know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So here, also, as if knowing nothing of such a sinner, He said, “Where have ye laid him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Similar in character was God’s voice in Paradise after man had sinned: “Adam, where art thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They say unto Him, Lord, come and see.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means this “see”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord sees when He pities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence it is said to Him, “Look upon my humility [affliction] and my pain, and forgive all my sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus wept.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said the Jews, Behold how He loved him!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Loved him,” what does that mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But some of them said, Could not this man, who opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not die?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He, who would do nought to hinder his dying, had something greater in view in raising him from the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus therefore again groaning in Himself, cometh to the tomb.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May His groaning have thee also for its object, if thou wouldst re-enter into life!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every man who lies in that dire moral condition has it said to him, “He cometh to the tomb.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It was a cave, and a stone had been laid upon it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dead under that stone, guilty under the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For you know that the law, which was given to the Jews, was inscribed on stone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And all the guilty are under the law: the right-living are in harmony with the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The law is not laid on a righteous man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What mean then the words, “Take ye away the stone”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Preach grace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Apostle Paul calleth himself a minister of the New Testament, not of the letter, but of the spirit; “for the letter,” he says, “killeth, but the spirit giveth life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The letter that killeth is like the stone that crusheth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Take ye away,” He saith, “the stone.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take away the weight of the law; preach grace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For if there had been a law given, which could have given life, verily righteousness should be by the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “take ye away the stone.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto Him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been [dead] four days.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus saith unto her, Have I not said unto thee, that, if thou believest, thou shalt see the glory of God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does He mean by this, “thou shalt see the glory of God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He can raise to life even one who is putrid and hath been four days [dead].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God; and, “Where sin abounded, grace also did superabound.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then they took away the stone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus lifted up His eyes, and said, Father, I thank Thee, that Thou hast heard me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And I knew that Thou hearest me always: but because of the people that stand by I said it, that they may believe that Thou hast sent me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when He had thus spoken, He cried with a loud voice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He groaned, He wept, He cried with a loud voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With what difficulty does one rise who lies crushed under the heavy burden of a habit of sinning!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet he does rise: he is quickened by hidden grace within; and after that loud voice he riseth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what followed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And immediately he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with bandages; and his face was bound about with a napkin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou wonder how he came forth with his feet bound, and wonderest not at this, that after four days’ interment he rose from the dead?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In both events it was the power of the Lord that operated, and not the strength of the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came forth, and yet still was bound.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Still in his burial shroud, he has already come outside the tomb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does it mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While thou despisest [Christ], thou liest in the arms of death; and if thy contempt reacheth the lengths I have mentioned, thou art buried as well: but when thou makest confession, thou comest forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is this coming forth, but the open acknowledgment thou makest of thy state, in quitting, as it were, the old refuges of darkness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the confession thou makest is effected by God, when He crieth with a loud voice, or in other words, calleth thee in abounding grace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when the dead man had come forth, still bound; confessing, yet guilty still; that his sins also might be taken away, the Lord said to His servants: “Loose him, and let him go.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does He mean by such words?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What soever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then many of the Jews who had come to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But some of them went away to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All of the Jews who had come to Mary did not believe, but many of them did.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But some of them,” whether of the Jews who had come, or of those who had believed, “went away to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done:” whether in the way of conveying intelligence, in order that they also might believe, or rather in the spirit of treachery, to arouse their anger.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But whoever were the parties, and whatever their motive, intelligence of these events was carried to the Pharisees.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they did not say, Let us believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these abandoned men were more occupied in considering what evil they could do to effect His ruin, than in consulting for their own preservation: and yet they were afraid, and took counsel of a kind together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “they said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles: if we let him thus alone, all men will believe on him; and the Romans shall come, and take away both our place and nation.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were afraid of losing their temporal possessions, and thought not of life eternal; and so they lost both.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Romans, after our Lord’s passion and entrance into glory, took from them both their place and nation, when they took the one by storm and transported the other: and now that also pursues them, which is said elsewhere, “But the children of the kingdom shall go into outer darkness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this was what they feared, that if all believed on Christ, there would be none remaining to defend the city of God and the temple against the Romans; just because they had a feeling that Christ’s teaching was directed against the temple itself and their own paternal laws.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And one of them, [named] Caiaphas, being the high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all, nor consider that it is expedient for us that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this spake he not of himself; but being high priest that year, he prophesied.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are here taught that the Spirit of prophecy used the agency even of wicked men to foretell what was future; which, however, the evangelist attributes to the divine sacramental fact that he was pontiff, which is to say, the high priest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It may, however, be a question in what way he is called the high priest of that year, seeing that God appointed one person to be high priest, who was to be succeeded only at his death by another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we are to understand that ambitious schemes and contentions among the Jews led to the appointment afterwards of more than one, and to their annual turn of service.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is said also of Zacharias: “And it came to pass that, while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his course, according to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to burn incense when he went into the temple of the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From which it is evident that there were more than one, and that each had his turn: for it was lawful for the high priest alone to place the incense on the altar.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And perhaps also there were several in actual service in the same year, who were succeeded next year by several others, and that it fell by lot to one of them to burn incense.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was it, then, that Caiaphas prophesied?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That Jesus should die for the nation; and not for the nation only, but that also He should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is added by the evangelist; for Caiaphas prophesied only of the Jewish nation, in which there were sheep of whom the Lord Himself had said, “I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the evangelist knew that there were other sheep, which were not of this fold, but which had also to be brought, that there might be one fold and one shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this was said in the way of predestination; for those who were still unbelieving were as yet neither His sheep nor the children of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then, from that day forth, they took counsel together for to put Him to death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with His disciples.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that there was any failure in His power, by which, had He only wished, He might have continued His intercourse with the Jews, and received no injury at their hands; but in His human weakness He furnished His disciples with an example of living, by which He might make it manifest that it was no sin in His believing ones, who are His members, to withdraw from the presence of their persecutors, and escape the fury of the wicked by concealment, rather than inflame it by showing themselves openly.
